summary:
Glass ionomer cements reinforced with metal particles exhibit significantly increased wear resistance due to their superior handling characteristics compared to conventional materials; however, the primary advantageous feature emphasized is heightened abrasion resistance which enhances longevity and performance in clinical applications.
The main advantage lies in greater wear resistance from metallic reinforcements, improving durability over time without compromising other critical aspects like fluoride release efficiency observed previously noted differences between types. This makes them suitable choices where prolonged exposure to mechanical stress occurs frequently necessitating robust restorations capable enduring harsh conditions typical found within oral
